title: Nojiko Reveals Nami’s Past
scene: On pages 5–8, Nojiko’s account of the past reveals how Nami, Bellemere, and Cocoyasi Village were damaged by Arlong’s arrival. Nojiko starts revealing the past that shaped Nami, Bellemere, and the village.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Belliemere! Belliemere!

dialogue.speaker: Child Nami
dialogue.text: Come out already! Your little cat burglar's done it again!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Jeez, you're noisy. What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Bellemere
dialogue.text: Oh, welcome home, Nami.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'm home, belliemere.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: You stay quiet!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Nami and Nojiko's foster mother BELLEMERE

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: For Heaven's sake, you need to properly discipline her.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Do you have any idea how many times she's done this now!? You're getting a terrible reputation among the townsfolk!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Oh what's the big deal, she's still just a kid.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'm sure you must've shoplifted once or twice too when you were a kid, right?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's precisely because of that attitude of yours that she's...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Alright, alright. Shanks again, anyways. You paid for her, didn't you?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I don't have any money on me right now so I'll pay you back next time...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: with my body. (1)

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Don't say such idiotic things!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What's this? Your face is all red. (1)

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'm sorry, Belliemere.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But I wanted this book no matter what!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: If you wanted it so much,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Then why didn't you just tell me?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: You're such a Klutz, Nami!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: If it were me, I'd have stolen it without getting caught.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: NO JIKO (8 years ago)

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Stealing is wrong!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Owww!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I was only joking!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But you wouldn't have bought it for me even if I said something!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Of course I would have. I do have enough money saved away to buy at least a book or two.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: In any case, the weather's been unusually good these days so all the orange farms have been able to harvest plenty of oranges.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That's why they're so cheap lately and why we're hardly selling any.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Understand?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Yeah...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Ohh, so this is a map of island? You've already moved on from drawing terrestrial maps to sea charts?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That's amazing.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Yup, and I'm studying navigation skills right now.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: 'Cause it's your dream, right?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Yup.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'm going to sail around the whole world using my navigation skills,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: And then make a map of the world based on what I saw with my own eyes!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: A map of the world, huh? I'll look forward to it!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Then I guess the map you drew today of this island is just one step towards that dream!

summary: This part deepens Nami’s backstory, showing how family, money, and survival became tied to Arlong’s control over the village.
